name: Thomas A. Dorsey
description: American musician, composer, and Christian evangelist influential in the development of early blues and 20th-century gospel music
abstract: Thomas Andrew Dorsey (July 1, 1899 – January 23, 1993) was an American musician, composer, and Christian evangelist influential in the development of early blues and 20th-century gospel music. He penned 3,000 songs, a third of them gospel, including "Take My Hand, Precious Lord" and "Peace in the Valley".

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Mahalia Jackson

works.t: Little Wooden Church on the Hill

Little Wooden Church on the Hill

about_quotes.speaker: Jang
about_quotes.evidence: I could relate that experience with the making of “Take My Hand Precious Lord” by Thomas A. Dorsey, who composed that — you know it’s one of the hymns of African- American people.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Jang
about_quotes.evidence: And it’s like — it’s like a river but not like a literate river... people like Thomas Dorsey, stories like him... So that gives me faith. And Thomas Dorsey had a lot of struggle in his life getting his music accepted in the churches... we have to look at that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Jang interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 17, 2003 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: Let’s just talk about the influence of gospel music and particularly with Thomas Dorsey being in Chicago at the time you were coming up. How did that work its way into the Dorothy Donegan style?
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Staton
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And you guys do some of the — what was it Thomas Dorsey that did some of the early — [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Thomas Dorsey.
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Staton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 12, 2008

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: But then Dorsey came in and gospel became very popular because it fell right in with the blues thing that was happening.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I met Thomas A. Dorsey, who wrote Precious Lord, Take My Hand
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
